content: Hey Saturday, I’m setting up a Discord bot for our Valorant community server and could use a plan. We’re on Discord.js v14 and I want a `!rank` command that pulls ELO from a PostgreSQL leaderboard.

Nice project—Discord.js v14 + Postgres is a solid stack for that. For `!rank`, have the command query a `players` table by Discord user ID and return ELO, tier, and maybe last-updated time. Add basic caching (like 30–60 seconds) so repeated lookups don’t hammer your DB during peak hours.
Makes sense. I also need a `!match` command for scheduling 5v5s and generating a simple bracket, ideally for Friday night events at 8:30 PM EST.

You can make `!match` create a match record (date/time, captains, team lists) and then generate a single-elim bracket JSON for the event channel. Use a signup window (like 24 hours) and lock rosters 15 minutes before 8:30 PM EST to avoid chaos. If you want, store bracket state in Postgres too so restarts don’t wipe progress.
Yep, persistence is important. I also want welcome messages that auto-assign a "Member" role, plus a mod-log channel that records bans, mutes, and message deletes.

Use the `guildMemberAdd` event for a welcome embed and immediately assign the Member role by role ID. For moderation logs, listen to events like `guildBanAdd`, timeout updates, and message deletions, then post structured embeds to `#mod-log` with moderator, target, reason, and timestamp. Keep your bot permissions tight so it can’t overreach outside required actions.
I’m hosting this on a $5 DigitalOcean droplet (Ubuntu). Any practical deployment tips so it stays up reliably?

On a $5 droplet, run Node 20 LTS with PM2 (`pm2 start bot.js --name valorbot`) and enable startup so it auto-recovers after reboot. Put secrets in a `.env` file, use UFW to allow only SSH + required ports, and add log rotation so disk usage stays under control. A nightly Postgres backup cron job is also worth it, even for a community server.
